steps.title: Read what the blank is doing
steps.content: The em dash before the blank sets off a phrase that explains the technical term "cognate replacement" just mentioned: "the rate at which a word will be replaced over time with a noncognate form." That's a definition/restatement of the term, not a new example or added fact.

steps.title: Pick the restatement transition
steps.content: "That is" is the standard transition for restatement or definition — it tells the reader the following phrase clarifies what the previous term means.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘for example,’: would introduce an instance of cognate replacement, but what follows defines the term rather than illustrating it. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘likewise,’: signals a parallel point, but no second comparable case is being added. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘in addition,’: signals an extra, separate point, but the phrase that follows is the same idea restated, not a new one. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘that is,’: signals restatement/definition, matching the explanatory phrase that follows. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
